quote: "The digital platform transformed what could have been a logistical nightmare into an effortless, editorial experience for our guests. It felt like every guest had their own personal concierge."
paragraphs: Managing an exclusive Italian getaway for a high-profile American couple requires more than just aesthetics. The challenge was orchestrating global guest logistics, ensuring each attendee felt like a VIP from the moment they landed in Naples.
With guests flying in from three continents, the digital experience needed to serve as a 24/7 concierge—translating the complexity of coastal transport into a seamless, high-end editorial journey.
A centralized digital dashboard providing guests with real-time flight tracking, personalized weather alerts for Ravello, and custom-tailored itineraries delivered via the wedding app.
Real-time API integration with major airlines to provide automated pickup notifications for the fleet team.
"The driver was waiting before I even cleared customs."

Through the bespoke 'Car Pickup' feature, guests could request transport with a single tap. The system intelligently pooled guests arriving on similar schedules, coordinating a fleet of luxury Mercedes and vintage Alfas.
An interactive map of the coast allowed guests to book exclusive, pre-vetted local experiences.
Private Faraglioni tour with champagne.
Traditional Ravello agriculture tour.
Exclusive rooftop dining reservation.
Let's design a digital experience that honors your most precious moments.
Limited availability for 2025/26

quote: "The team at Still didn’t just build us a website; they built us a digital legacy asset. It was the heart of our wedding experience from the first email to the last photo upload."
paragraphs: "We wanted our guests to feel the salt in the air before they even boarded their flights. The Still team transformed our minimalist aesthetic into a digital platform that managed every detail while feeling like a luxury editorial piece."
Seamless experiences crafted for the discerning traveler.
Discover the native blossoms of Santorini that inspired our floral arrangements...

Exclusive access to private balconies for photography.
Hidden workshops where guests picked up custom linens.

quote: "The digital platform didn’t just simplify our planning; it elevated the entire guest journey. We were able to tell our story and share our commitment to sustainability without sacrificing a single ounce of elegance."
paragraphs: Nature-Centric & Sustainable Design
For Astrid and Nils, luxury was defined not by excess, but by intention. Their vision was a celebration that honored the Swedish landscape without leaving a trace.
We approached this project with a digital-first philosophy, replacing traditional heavy-stock paper invites with a sophisticated "Paperless Portal" and dynamic mobile-optimized itineraries.
Crafting a seamless, paperless guest experience.
A high-fidelity digital hub where guests can view their bespoke invitations, manage RSVPs, and select seasonal menu preferences—all without a single sheet of paper.
Stockholm exploration curated for the eco-conscious guest. Highlighting electric ferry routes and bike rentals.
II. The Orchard Feast
III. Sculptural Details
Let’s craft a digital experience that reflects your values and preserves your most cherished memories.
© 2024 Still. Sustainably Curated.
Bridging continents through digital hospitality. Layla and Omar envisioned a wedding that didn’t just invite their global friends to Cairo, but immersed them in its rich history.

The goal was to create a digital bridge—a singular destination that offered a warm, Egyptian-inspired welcome while simplifying the complexities of international travel. We focused on reducing the friction of destination wedding logistics through an editorial-first guest portal.

An interactive sightseeing guide designed as a timeless record. Guests were able to explore Cairo's legendary landmarks—from the Giza plateau to the hidden cafes of Al-Muizz Street—all curated by the couple.
We developed a bespoke travel module that provided real-time weather alerts, visa guidance, and cultural etiquette tips for Cairo’s international visitors.
Predictive weather mapping to ensure guests packed for Cairo's specific daytime heat and cool desert evenings.
Integrated audio clips of Egyptian Arabic greetings, allowing guests to feel confident in local interactions.
A live-updating guide for Cairo's transportation, including secure booking links for private car services.

"The team at Still transformed our wedding logistics into a journey of discovery. Our global guests didn't just attend a wedding; they experienced our heritage through the most beautiful digital lens imaginable."
Let’s create a bespoke digital experience that reflects your unique love story and cultural heritage.
© 2024 Still. Curating Eternal Moments.

quote: "The team at Still captured the soul of our wedding before it even happened. Our guests were mesmerized by the digital experience."
paragraphs: For Sophie and Marc, Paris wasn't just a destination; it was a character in their story. We designed a bespoke digital invitation system that mirrored the city's effortless style—minimalist layouts, high-contrast typography, and interactive RSVPs that felt like unfolding a personal letter in a Marais café.
Curated Digital Artistry
An interactive digital guestbook where loved ones from across the globe shared handwritten notes and voice recordings, later compiled into a bespoke physical archive.
A hand-drawn, interactive map guiding guests through a curated trail of Sophie & Marc's favorite hidden Parisian corners and event venues.
A sophisticated digital vault for post-wedding photography, curated with a rhythmic, editorial flow to preserve the magic forever.
Every story is unique. Let us curate yours with the elegance it deserves.

quote: "The team at Still didn't just build us a website; they built us a digital keepsake."
paragraphs: For Elena and James, the wedding was an exercise in restraint and purposeful beauty. Our goal was to translate their high-fashion aesthetic into a digital platform that felt as tactile as their letterpress invitations.
